summary: free-anthropic-claude is a brand-impersonation dropper that fetches and executes a crypto-wallet stealer at install time
details: The package is published as 'free-anthropic-claude' and self-describes as an 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', but its README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' in-memory cache and the only exported functionality is a thin Map-backed cache — the Anthropic Claude branding exists solely as a lure. On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook invokes `createCache()` which schedules `_prefetch()` after a randomized 3–7s delay. `_prefetch` POSTs an auth token over HTTPS (with `rejectUnauthorized: false` to disable TLS verification) to one of four hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoints — 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146, all on port 8443 — base64-decodes the response payload, writes the decoded files to disguised paths (`~/.local/share/com.apple.sync`, `~/.local/share/prometheus`, `AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows Security\Health`), and executes `main.py` via Python. If no usable Python interpreter is found, `_deploy` bootstraps one: it downloads Miniconda from `repo.anaconda.com` on Linux, runs `winget install` Python 3.12 on Windows, or `brew install python3` on macOS, then pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, and `eth-account` — a dependency set that fingerprints cryptocurrency seed-phrase / private-key theft — and spawns `main.py` detached. On macOS, the same flow shells out to `sqlite3` against `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` to `INSERT OR REPLACE` rows granting `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, the running `node` binary, and `/usr/bin/python3`, self-granting Full Disk Access and bypassing macOS privacy controls without user consent. Every sensitive identifier is constructed by joining string arrays at runtime (`['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['exec','Sync'].join('')`, `module[['constr','uctor'].join('')]['_l'+'oad']`) to evade static analysis. cli.js wraps `createCache()` in an empty try/catch and `setTimeout(..., 180000)` — a 3-minute keep-alive whose own comment admits 'pip install can take 2min', confirming the author's awareness that pip is being invoked from a postinstall.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
